design_philosophy.core_loop: Select optimal destruction order of grid-based objects with a hammer to clear levels while avoiding enemies; figure out spatial puzzle solutions under time pressure
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Irreversible object destruction creates puzzle dependency—destroying something too early makes levels unwinnable; enemy pressure (Spike, fireballs) creates time scarcity
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Controlled demolition expert methodically dismantling structures; puzzle-solving through destruction rather than platforming acrobatics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Removes jumping (Mario's signature mechanic) to enforce puzzle-first gameplay; grid-based destruction order creates consequence-heavy decision making; level editor enables player-generated content at design time

difficulty.lives_system: Start with 5 lives; lose one life on enemy/fireball contact; game over when all lives depleted
difficulty.continues_system: Player can abort any level at will and restart; can select any level from 1-100 from title screen
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpointing; completing a level advances progression, no mid-level checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: 100 levels available from start, player selects entry point; can abort and retry without penalty beyond time investment; difficulty scales across level progression

progression.unlock_mechanism: All 100 levels accessible from title screen immediately; player chooses starting level rather than forced progression
replayability_hooks: Level editor allows creation and design of 4 custom levels
Bonus point scoring for destruction chains (multiple objects in sequence)
Bonus items scattered in levels for collection
VS. mode (arcade) with simultaneous two-player gameplay

world_building_techniques: Spike introduced as antagonistic construction foreman character disrupting demolition work
Mario reimagined without jumping ability due to hammer weight—mechanical constraint implies narrative context
Grid-based construction site aesthetic establishes demolition/deconstruction theme

boss_design.philosophy: No traditional boss encounters; Spike serves as persistent mobile hazard rather than climactic boss—represents environmental threat that increases pressure throughout levels
notable_bosses: Spike (construction foreman antagonist, known as Blackie in Japanese version)—chases Mario, knocks down objects, causes unintended falls; persistent threat across multiple levels rather than single encounter

faction_indicators: Mario as construction worker dismantling site (labor/skilled worker)
Spike as disruptive foreman antagonist (management/authority conflict)
danger_signals: Irreversible level states—poor planning leads to unwinnable situations requiring abort/restart
Spike's harassment mechanic mirrors workplace disruption/sabotage
Time pressure from enemy movement creates stress conditions
environmental_storytelling: Grid-based construction site with destructible/indestructible materials establishes working-class demolition context
Barrels (indestructible) vs. walls/pillars (destructible) imply structural hierarchy
Doors mechanism for enemy containment suggests safety/hazard management systems
